contents.value:
```zsh
# Regular alias:
alias ll='ls -alF'
alias gs='git status'
alias dc='docker compose'

# Global alias (expanded anywhere in line):
alias -g G='| grep'
alias -g L='| less'
alias -g NUL='> /dev/null 2>&1'

# Suffix alias (run when opening file by extension):
alias -s json='code'
alias -s log='tail -f'
alias -s py='python3'

# Usage:
ps aux G nginx       # expands to: ps aux | grep nginx
./data.json          # opens in VS Code
```
**alias** creates shortcuts. Regular (-r, default) replaces first word. Global (-g) expands anywhere. Suffix (-s) sets handler for file extensions.

```zsh
# Most useful options:
setopt AUTO_CD              # Type dir name to cd
setopt AUTO_PUSHD           # cd pushes to dir stack
setopt GLOB_DOTS            # Include dotfiles in globs
setopt EXTENDED_GLOB        # ^, ~, # glob operators
setopt NO_CASE_GLOB         # Case-insensitive matching
setopt CORRECT              # Suggest corrections
setopt INTERACTIVE_COMMENTS # Allow # comments
setopt PROMPT_SUBST         # Expand variables in prompt

# History options:
setopt SHARE_HISTORY        # Sync across sessions
setopt HIST_IGNORE_ALL_DUPS # Remove older duplicates
setopt HIST_IGNORE_SPACE    # Skip space-prefixed cmds
setopt HIST_VERIFY          # Show before executing !!

# Disable an option:
unsetopt BEEP
setopt NO_BEEP  # equivalent
```
**setopt** enables shell options. Options can be written NO_BEEP or NOBEEP or nobeep. Use `setopt` alone to list all enabled options.

```zsh
# Autoload marks functions for lazy loading from fpath:
autoload -Uz compinit && compinit  # Completion system
autoload -Uz vcs_info              # VCS info for prompt
autoload -Uz add-zsh-hook          # Hook management
autoload -Uz colors && colors      # Color variables
autoload -Uz zmv                   # Advanced rename
autoload -Uz zargs                 # xargs equivalent
autoload -Uz zcalc                 # Calculator

# Create your own autoloaded function:
# 1. Create file ~/.zsh/functions/myfunc
# 2. Write function body (no function wrapper needed)
# 3. Add to fpath:
fpath=(~/.zsh/functions $fpath)
autoload -Uz myfunc
```
**autoload -Uz** is the standard way to load functions. `-U` suppresses alias expansion, `-z` forces zsh-style loading. Functions are loaded from `$fpath` on first call.

```zsh
# Prompt escape sequences:
# %n  username       %m  hostname
# %~  current dir    %d  full path
# %#  # if root      %?  exit status
# %D  date           %*  time HH:MM:SS
# %j  number of jobs

# Colors:
# %F{red}text%f      foreground color
# %K{blue}text%k     background color
# %B bold %b         %U underline %u

# Examples:
PROMPT='%F{cyan}%n%f@%F{green}%m%f:%F{yellow}%~%f %# '
RPROMPT='%F{gray}%*%f'

# Git info in prompt:
autoload -Uz vcs_info
precmd() { vcs_info }
zstyle ':vcs_info:git:*' formats '(%b)'
setopt PROMPT_SUBST
PROMPT='%~ ${vcs_info_msg_0_} %# '
```
**PROMPT / PS1** sets the primary prompt. Use `%F{color}...%f` for colors. Enable `PROMPT_SUBST` for variable expansion in prompts.

```zsh
# List all bindings:
bindkey

# Emacs mode (default):
bindkey -e

# Vi mode:
bindkey -v

# Common bindings:
bindkey '^R'    history-incremental-search-backward
bindkey '^S'    history-incremental-search-forward
bindkey '^P'    up-line-or-history
bindkey '^N'    down-line-or-history
bindkey '^A'    beginning-of-line
bindkey '^E'    end-of-line
bindkey '^K'    kill-line
bindkey '^W'    backward-kill-word
bindkey '^[[A'  up-line-or-search        # Up arrow
bindkey '^[[B'  down-line-or-search      # Down arrow
bindkey '^[[3~' delete-char              # Delete key

# Show what a key does:
bindkey '^R'
```
**bindkey** maps keys to ZLE (Zsh Line Editor) widgets. Use `^X` for Ctrl+X, `^[` for Alt/Escape. `bindkey -l` lists keymaps.

```zsh
# Completion styling:
zstyle ':completion:*' menu select              # Tab menu
zstyle ':completion:*' matcher-list \            
    'm:{a-z}={A-Z}' 'r:|[._-]=* r:|=*'         # Case insensitive
zstyle ':completion:*' list-colors ${(s.:.)LS_COLORS}  # Colorize
zstyle ':completion:*' use-cache on              # Cache completions
zstyle ':completion:*' cache-path ~/.zsh/cache
zstyle ':completion:*' group-name ''             # Group by type
zstyle ':completion:*:descriptions' format '%F{yellow}-- %d --%f'
zstyle ':completion:*:warnings' format '%F{red}No matches%f'

# Kill command completion:
zstyle ':completion:*:*:kill:*:processes' list-colors '=(#b) #([0-9]#)*=0=01;31'
zstyle ':completion:*:kill:*' command 'ps -u $USER -o pid,%cpu,tty,cmd'

# SSH host completion:
zstyle ':completion:*:ssh:*' hosts $(awk '/^Host / {print $2}' ~/.ssh/config)
```
**zstyle** configures the completion system. Pattern format: `:completion:function:completer:command:argument:tag`. Incredibly powerful for customizing tab completion behavior.

```zsh
# Basic globs:
*           # Any file
**/*        # Recursive
*.{js,ts}   # Alternation

# Glob qualifiers (after closing paren):
ls *(.)     # Regular files only
ls *(/)     # Directories only
ls *(@)     # Symlinks only
ls *(*)     # Executables
ls *(om)    # Sort by modification time
ls *(oL)    # Sort by size
ls *(Lk+100)  # Files > 100KB
ls *(mh-1)    # Modified in last hour
ls *(mw-1)    # Modified in last week
ls *(.om[1,5]) # 5 most recent files
ls *(N)     # Null glob (no error if empty)

# Extended glob (setopt EXTENDED_GLOB):
ls ^*.bak    # NOT matching *.bak
ls *.txt~secret*  # *.txt except secret*
ls (#i)*.jpg      # Case-insensitive
```
**Zsh globbing** is extremely powerful. Glob qualifiers filter by file type, size, date, permissions. Extended glob adds negation (^), exclusion (~), case-insensitive (#i).

```zsh
# Configuration:
HISTFILE=~/.zsh_history
HISTSIZE=50000
SAVEHIST=50000

# Essential options:
setopt SHARE_HISTORY          # Share between sessions
setopt HIST_IGNORE_ALL_DUPS   # Remove older duplicate
setopt HIST_IGNORE_SPACE      # Space prefix = private
setopt HIST_VERIFY            # Show before executing !!
setopt INC_APPEND_HISTORY     # Write immediately

# History expansion:
!!          # Last command
!$          # Last argument
!^          # First argument
!*          # All arguments
!-2         # Two commands ago
!cmd        # Last cmd starting with 'cmd'
^old^new    # Replace in last command

# History search:
bindkey '^R' history-incremental-search-backward
bindkey '^[[A' up-line-or-search  # Up arrow
```
**History** in zsh is highly configurable. Use `SHARE_HISTORY` for multi-session sync. Space-prefix commands are ignored with `HIST_IGNORE_SPACE`.

```zsh
# Basic:
${var}           # Value
${#var}          # Length
${var:-default}  # Default if unset
${var:=default}  # Assign default
${var:+alt}      # Alternative if set
${var:?error}    # Error if unset

# Substring:
${var:offset:len}  # Substring
${var#pat}   # Remove shortest prefix
${var##pat}  # Remove longest prefix
${var%pat}   # Remove shortest suffix
${var%%pat}  # Remove longest suffix
${var/pat/rep}   # Replace first
${var//pat/rep}  # Replace all

# Zsh-specific flags:
${(U)var}    # UPPERCASE
${(L)var}    # lowercase
${(C)var}    # Capitalize Words
${(s:.:)var} # Split on '.'
${(j:,:)arr} # Join with ','
${(u)arr}    # Unique elements
${(o)arr}    # Sort
${(f)var}    # Split on newlines
${(F)arr}    # Join with newlines
${(k)hash}   # Keys of assoc array
${(v)hash}   # Values of assoc array
```
**Parameter expansion** is zsh's most powerful feature. The `(flags)` syntax provides string/array transformations without external commands.

```zsh
# Initialize:
autoload -Uz compinit && compinit

# Custom completion function:
_myapp() {
    local -a commands
    commands=(
        'start:Start the application'
        'stop:Stop the application'
        'deploy:Deploy to production'
    )
    
    _arguments \
        '-v[Verbose output]' \
        '-c[Config file]:config file:_files -g "*.yml"' \
        '1:command:->cmd' && return
    
    case $state in
        cmd) _describe 'command' commands ;;
    esac
}
compdef _myapp myapp

# Useful completion functions:
# _files      - complete files
# _directories - complete directories
# _users      - complete usernames
# _hosts      - complete hostnames
# _describe   - describe and complete from array
# _arguments  - full argument specification
```
**Completion system** provides intelligent tab completion. Write `_command` functions using `_arguments` for option specs and `_describe` for subcommands.

```zsh
# Install:
sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"

# Configuration in ~/.zshrc:
export ZSH="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh"
ZSH_THEME="robbyrussell"  # or: agnoster, powerlevel10k

plugins=(
    git           # Git aliases (gst, gco, gp, gl)
    docker        # Docker completions
    kubectl       # k8s completions & aliases
    z             # Frecency-based cd
    fzf           # Fuzzy finder integration
    colored-man-pages
    command-not-found
    zsh-autosuggestions
    zsh-syntax-highlighting
)

source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh

# Custom plugins: ~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/
# Custom themes: ~/.oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/

# Update: omz update
# List plugins: ls ~/.oh-my-zsh/plugins/
```
**Oh My Zsh** is a framework for managing zsh configuration. 300+ plugins and 150+ themes. Use sparingly — too many plugins slow startup.

```zsh
autoload -Uz zmv

# Rename with patterns:
zmv '(*).txt' '$1.md'          # .txt -> .md
zmv '(*).JPG' '${(L)1}.jpg'   # UPPERCASE -> lowercase
zmv -W '*.jpeg' '*.jpg'        # Simple wildcard rename

# Complex renames:
zmv '(**/)(*).txt' '$1${2// /_}.txt'  # Replace spaces
zmv -n '(*).log' '$1_$(date +%Y%m%d).log'  # Dry run (-n)

# Copy / Link variants:
zcp '*.txt' backup/             # Copy with patterns
zln -s '*.conf' links/          # Symlink with patterns
```
**zmv** is a powerful batch rename tool. Use `-n` for dry run. `-W` enables simpler wildcard syntax. Supports all zsh parameter expansion.

```zsh
# Hook functions run at specific times:
precmd()  { }  # Before each prompt
preexec() { }  # Before each command ($1=typed, $2=expanded)
chpwd()   { }  # After directory change
periodic(){ }  # Every $PERIOD seconds
zshaddhistory() { }  # Before adding to history

# Multiple functions per hook:
autoload -Uz add-zsh-hook
add-zsh-hook precmd my_precmd_1
add-zsh-hook precmd my_precmd_2
add-zsh-hook -d precmd my_precmd_1  # Remove

# Example: Timer for commands
__timer_preexec() { __timer=$EPOCHREALTIME }
__timer_precmd() {
    [[ -n $__timer ]] || return
    local elapsed=$(( EPOCHREALTIME - __timer ))
    (( elapsed > 3 )) && printf 'Elapsed: %.2fs\n' $elapsed
    unset __timer
}
add-zsh-hook preexec __timer_preexec
add-zsh-hook precmd __timer_precmd
```
**Hook functions** execute at shell events. Use `add-zsh-hook` to register multiple handlers. `preexec` receives the command as arguments.
